reportedDefect: Frames 002-004: bow torn / grip hand missing / bow appears floating; discontinuity between hand and bow.

step1_defectIsInSource.method: Directly opened source PNGs hero.archer_attack_002/003/004.png (not the contact sheet).
step1_defectIsInSource.finding: Defect is present in the source frames themselves. The golden bow is fragmented into displaced blocks with a detached, floating upper limb (top-right) and hard rectangular mesh-warp shear seams across the right torso/arm. Grip hand is lost. Confirmed NOT a contact-sheet rendering artifact.

step2_defectExtent.method: Per-pixel abs-diff of frames 002/003/004 vs byte-clean neighbor frame 001, thresholded >30.
step2_defectExtent.finding: All damage sits in the upper-right quadrant (right shoulder, right arm, cape, and bow).

step3_criticalFinding.method: Split the 001-vs-002/003/004 diff into INSIDE the damaged region (x[245-585] y[115-610]) vs OUTSIDE it.
step3_criticalFinding.finding: OUTSIDE the torn-bow region, frames 002/003/004 are BYTE-IDENTICAL to frame 001 (0 changed pixels). The ENTIRE frame-to-frame difference across the draw phase is the bow-tearing warp. There is NO body, arm, shoulder, or drawing-hand motion beneath the defect.
step3_criticalFinding.implication: The reported 'torn bow' is not a flaw layered on top of a good draw pose. The torn bow IS the entire content of the draw-phase 'animation'. The mesh-warp produced only progressive tearing of the bow region and nothing else.

step4_fixAttempt.method: Deterministic region-restore: composite the clean bow + grip-hand + upper-right body from frame 001 into the damaged region of 002/003/004, feathered at boundaries that already match.
step4_fixAttempt.result: Removes the defect cleanly (bow intact, grip continuous, no seams, identity/costume/proportions preserved exactly).
step4_fixAttempt.sideEffect: Because the only difference from frame 001 WAS the damaged region, restoring it makes fixed 002/003/004 byte-identical to frame 001. The draw phase becomes fully static and frames 001/002/003/004/006 become five identical frames.
step4_fixAttempt.verdict: This removes the torn-bow FAIL but re-introduces the NEAR_STATIC FAIL (the exact reason Attack v1 was rejected). Not a genuine fix.

rootCause: The v2 production method (pose-based mesh warp, generate-archer-attack-v2-articulated-v1.py) displaces Neutral Master pixels in the bow/upper-right region. For the draw-phase frames the displacement magnitude was large enough to shear the bow into disconnected blocks and detach its upper limb, while producing NO coherent re-posing of the arm/hand/body. The standing Neutral Master has the archer holding the bow relaxed at her side; a readable archery draw (bow arm extended, drawing hand pulling the string toward the face) is a genuine RE-POSE that cannot be synthesized from the standing asset by geometric pixel displacement without either (a) tearing, or (b) collapsing back to the source pose.
whyNotPatchable: A genuinely readable draw requires re-posing limbs (raising/extending the bow arm and bringing the drawing hand to the string). That needs a rigged puppet reconstruction or a pose-guided image-generation/inpainting model. Neither is available as a deterministic pixel operation in this environment. Any deterministic warp that is large enough to READ as a draw is precisely what tears the bow; any warp small enough to avoid tearing collapses to the static source pose.
decision: Per user instruction (2026-07-19): ESCALATE to the re-pose method rather than ship a defect-removal that produces a static/duplicate result. Production frames 002-004 left UNTOUCHED (baseline hashes preserved). No fake fix committed.

requirements: Preserve approved Archer identity (face, hood, hair, ears, eyes, green/gold costume, ornate bow design) from the Neutral Master.
Do NOT lock the body pose: allow the bow arm to extend and the drawing hand to reach the string across the draw phase.
Produce distinct, readable key poses: ready -> raise -> nock -> draw-mid -> full-draw -> release -> follow-through -> recovery -> neutral exit.
Keep canvas 640x960, anchor [0.5,0.92], 12 FPS, projectileRelease marker semantics, feet pin y>=800, and frame-9 byte-identical Neutral exit.

Needs an image-generation/inpainting capability (not available deterministically in this environment) OR user-supplied re-posed frames (the pattern already used for other characters' motion-test imports).
recommendedNextMethod.note: This mirrors the 'distinct articulated key-pose reconstruction / pose-guided regeneration' path already flagged in docs/reviews/archer-v2-final-visual-review.json as the fallback if visual review rejected the mesh-warp candidate.

statusFlags.note: Unchanged. No approval granted. No validator threshold modified.
